description: Rules proposed automatically by the Phase LEARN pipeline based on trending rubric-criterion failures. Every rule here was drafted by an LLM, then human-reviewed and approved before landing. Empty by design until the first proposal is approved.

guidelines.rule: When designing a UI that presents a collection of distinct, self-contained entities, such as recipes, team members, blog posts, or pricing plans, utilize card components to visually group and present each entity clearly.
guidelines.why: The generator consistently scores 0/2 on CARDS_JUSTIFIED, with a 71% fail rate across 7 runs, indicating a failure to appropriately use or justify card components for collections of distinct items.
examples: BAD: A 'recipe card collection' app that displays recipes as a simple bulleted list or uses cards that are visually empty or lack meaningful content.
GOOD: A 'recipe card collection' app that displays each recipe within a distinct card, featuring an image, title, and summary, making each recipe a clear, scannable unit.
